questions.question: Briefly describe two communication protocols used in home automation and state one advantage of each.
keyPoints: Zigbee: Low power consumption, ideal for battery-operated sensors
WiFi: High bandwidth, direct integration with existing home networks
BLE: Ubiquitous smartphone support for easy user interaction

questions.question: Using the example of a smart thermostat, explain the four stages of the IoT lifecycle.
keyPoints: Sensing: Temperature sensor collects room temperature data
Communication: Data transmitted via WiFi/Zigbee to hub or cloud
Processing: Data analyzed against user preferences and schedules
Actuation: Command sent to HVAC system to adjust temperature, completing the feedback loop

questions.question: Compare and contrast smart home IoT systems with precision agriculture IoT systems across four key dimensions.
keyPoints: Scale: Home is single building vs agriculture covers large geographical areas
Communication: Home uses short-range WiFi/Zigbee vs agriculture uses long-range LoRaWAN
Power: Home devices are mains-powered vs field sensors need battery/solar
Data volume: Home is event-driven (moderate) vs agriculture is continuous monitoring (high)

questions.question: Design a comprehensive smart home lighting system discussing technical, UX, and security considerations.
keyPoints: Technical: Protocol selection, hub/gateway architecture, latency requirements
UX: Mobile app control, voice assistant integration, automation routines
Security: Encryption, secure boot, firmware updates, strong authentication
Challenges: Setup complexity, power requirements, privacy concerns
